Home/Counties/St. Johns County FHA Loans
St. Johns County FHA Loans

FHA loans in St. Johns County, Florida

FHA loans across St. Johns County with as little as 3.5% down. Here's the county's 2026 FHA loan limit, where FHA fits in this fast-growing, top-rated county, and how to get pre-approved.

St. Johns County Home Loans

FHA loans in St. Johns County, Florida

St. Johns County, home to historic St. Augustine and affluent Ponte Vedra, is one of Florida's fastest-growing counties and consistently ranks among its best for schools. That popularity has driven home prices up and fueled massive new-construction communities like Nocatee. FHA buyers do well by focusing on the county's abundant new construction and more attainable areas, where the loan limit applies.

St. Johns County FHA limit (1-unit)$541,287

2026 single-family

Minimum down3.5%

With a 580+ score

Min credit score580

500–579 with 10% down

Property types1–4 units

Owner-occupied

What the FHA limit means in St. Johns County

With the 2026 FHA floor of $541,287 and FHA's 3.5% minimum down, you can finance a St. Johns County home priced up to roughly $560,000 (your loan covers the price minus your down payment). That covers much of St. Johns County's new construction and mid-market homes, though luxury Ponte Vedra and coastal properties often exceed it. A handful of Florida counties are set higher, so we confirm St. Johns County's exact figure with HUD.

Top schools, new construction, and where FHA fits

St. Johns is a new-construction powerhouse — communities around St. Augustine and the Nocatee area offer modern homes, many within the FHA limit, ideal for first-time buyers drawn by the schools. Luxury Ponte Vedra and beachfront homes often exceed FHA limits, so we'll help you target the homes that fit your budget. New construction passes the FHA appraisal easily.

Communities We Serve

FHA loans across St. Johns County

We help FHA buyers throughout St. Johns County, including St. Augustine, Ponte Vedra, and the Nocatee area. Wherever you are in the county, we will explain your FHA loan limit, rate, and monthly payment, and get you pre-approved fast. Start with our first-time buyer guide or estimate your payment with the FHA calculator.

Down payment help in St. Johns County

You don't have to cover the down payment alone. FHA allows gift funds, sellers can contribute up to 6% toward closing costs, and St. Johns County buyers can often pair FHA with assistance from the Florida Housing Finance Corporation and St. Johns County's local SHIP-funded programs. Funding and eligibility change frequently, so we'll help you confirm what's currently available — see our FHA down payment assistance guide.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in St. Johns County for 2026?+
St. Johns County is at the 2026 Florida FHA floor of $541,287. Confirm the exact figure with HUD's official lookup.
Where does FHA work best in St. Johns County?+
In the county's abundant new construction (including the Nocatee area) and more attainable communities; luxury Ponte Vedra and beachfront homes often exceed FHA limits.
Does FHA work for new-construction homes in St. Johns County?+
Yes — the area's plentiful new construction typically passes the FHA appraisal easily and, when priced within the limit, works well with FHA.
How much down payment do I need in St. Johns County?+
As little as 3.5% with a 580+ score, reduced with gift funds and Florida assistance.
Do you serve all of St. Johns County?+
Yes — St. Augustine, Ponte Vedra, the Nocatee area, and every community in the county.
